name: OpenPGP
author: Bulwark Mail Community
description: End-to-end OpenPGP (GnuPG-compatible) for webmail: generate, import, export and manage public and private keys; encrypt, decrypt and sign messages; and automatically verify signatures and decrypt incoming PGP/MIME and inline-PGP mail. Private keys are stored encrypted at rest and unlocked into memory only for the current session, so secret material never leaves your browser. Runs in the privileged (same-origin) plugin tier with a bundled openpgp.js engine.

messageFormat.label: Outgoing message format
messageFormat.description: PGP/MIME (RFC 3156) is the modern standard. Inline PGP places the armor directly in the message body for compatibility with older clients, but cannot protect attachments.

defaultEncryptionAlgorithm.label: Preferred symmetric cipher
defaultEncryptionAlgorithm.description: Session-key cipher used to encrypt message bodies. AES-256 is recommended.

newKeyType.label: Algorithm for newly generated keys
newKeyType.description: ECC (curve25519) keys are small and fast; RSA-4096 maximises compatibility with older clients.

signByDefault.label: Sign outgoing mail by default
signByDefault.description: When a personal key is unlocked, pre-enable the Sign toggle in the composer.

encryptByDefault.label: Encrypt outgoing mail when possible
encryptByDefault.description: Pre-enable the Encrypt toggle when a public key is known for every recipient.

autoImportSignerKeys.label: Auto-save signer keys
autoImportSignerKeys.description: When an incoming signed message carries the signer's public key, remember it for future encryption.

lockOnLogout.label: Lock keys on logout
lockOnLogout.description: Wipe all unlocked private keys from memory when you sign out or switch accounts.

warnOnUnverifiedSender.label: Warn on unverified signer
warnOnUnverifiedSender.description: Show a caution banner when a signature is valid but the signing key is not in your keyring.

enableKeyserverLookup.label: Use a public keyserver
enableKeyserverLookup.description: Enable the 'Find a key' tool (fetch and fingerprint-verify recipient keys from keys.openpgp.org) and the 'Publish' button (upload your own public key so others can find it). Lookups and uploads only happen when you explicitly ask; your secret key is never uploaded.

keyserverUrl.label: Keyserver URL
keyserverUrl.description: Base URL of the Verifying Keyserver (VKS) used for key lookups. Defaults to keys.openpgp.org, which only publishes email addresses their owners have verified. A different host must also be added to the plugin's httpOrigins allowlist in the manifest.

blockRemoteContent.label: Block remote images & content
blockRemoteContent.description: Stop decrypted messages from loading remote images, styles and other external resources. Blocks tracking pixels and read receipts. Inline (embedded) images still display.

disableExternalLinks.label: Disable external links
disableExternalLinks.description: Render links to external websites as inert, non-clickable text to prevent accidental navigation or phishing. When off, external links open in a new tab with no referrer.

en.banner.locked: Encrypted — unlock your key to read
en.settings.title: OpenPGP keys
